violations.description: Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. > Dough in plastic bag discovered to be on the floor in the walk in cooler. This needs to be off of the ground, elevated.

Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Raw animal food was not properly separated from raw or cooked ready-to-eat food. > Found raw philly cheese steak meat stored over ready to eat foods in walk in cooler.
Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --No cloth for sanitizer use was prepared during inspection.

Cooling of potentially hazardous food. — --Cooked potentially hazardous food was not cooled properly from 140F to 70F or below within two hours. > Discovered recently cooked philly cheese steak meat in pizza station prep cooler. Measured at 155 degrees F. When asked how long ago it was put in there I was told an hour. Meat would not have cooled to appropriate temperature without intervention.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. > Prepared nacho cheese was not labeled or date marked in prep cooler on the pizza prep line.

Clean-in-place (CIP) equipment - Design and construction. — --Food contact surface of clean in place equipment was not cleaned properly. > Clean in place mixer had not been cleaned from the previous day's shift when asked. This must be cleaned regularly and adequately when necessary.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. > Dough preparation area had not been wiped down during operation with a sanitizing cloth. When asked, it had not yet been done during the day.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. > Large amount of food accumulation caked into the conveyor belt on pizza oven. This needs to be cleaned much more frequently. > Walk in cooler ceiling and fan are in need of cleaning.--Ventilation hood system was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. > Ventilation hood system has not been cleaned for some time, lots of grease accumulation in the filters.
Manual cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Spray nozzle of three compartment sink descends into the sink. It must not extend past the rim of the sink.

Storage of cleaned equipment and utensils. — --Clean equipment/utensil was not stored in a clean, dry manner to protect from contamination. > Utensils and equipment cleaned the previous night were sitting on the drainboard of the sink.
Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was reused or used more than one time. > Plastic container meant for having sauces being reused to store philly cheese steak meat.
Toilet facilities. — --Toilet room used by women did not contain at least one covered waste receptacle. > Provide a covered waste receptacle for the women's restroom.

Lighting. — --Light intensity for walk-in refrigeration or food storage area was insufficient. > The lighting in the walk in cooler is very dim and dark. Change bulb and/or provide better lighting for walk in cooler.

Handling and storage of poisonous and toxic materials. — --Cleaning, sanitizing or drying agents were not adequately stored separate from poisonous, toxic materials or pesticides. > Sanitizer for food contact surface stored next to degreaser and glass cleaner. > Spray bottle labeled "Glass cleaner" is being used to store dish soap.
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Hot holding and cold holding temperatures maintained, unwrapped food not reserved (5 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (2 pts deducted)
Provided as required, designed, constructed, installed, maintained, and cleaned (1 pt deducted)
